questionHtml:
At which temperature could ΔH, ΔS, and ΔG all be positive?

A.  High temperatures

B.  Low temperatures

C.  Any temperature

D.  No temperature

meta.title: At which temperature could ΔH, ΔS, and ΔG all be positive? A. High temperatures B. Low temperatures C. Any temperature D. No temperature
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 1.4.2—Change in Gibbs energy, ΔG, relates the energy that can be obtained from a chemical reaction to the change in enthalpy, ΔH, change in entropy, ΔS, and absolute temperature, T. Apply the equation ΔG⦵ = ΔH⦵ − TΔS⦵ to calculate unknown values of these terms.
